fact: January 30 is exactly 30 days into the year, which means you've already lived through 1/12th of the entire year!
The sun rises about 1 minute and 20 seconds earlier than it did just one week ago in northern regions - that's like gaining a whole commercial break of extra morning light!
January 30 falls in the middle of Aquarius season, making it one of the most 'watery' days of the year according to astrology, even though it's winter in many places.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, January 30 is typically peak summer, when cicadas often make sounds so loud they can be heard from far away!
Tree sap in cold northern regions is usually at its thickest around this date because freezing temperatures turn it sluggish, like nature's own syrup.
January 30 is exactly 55 days before the spring equinox, putting it at the spot where winter is definitely losing its grip but spring hasn't quite arrived yet.
In many northern cities, January 30 often marks about one month since December snow first accumulated, creating those crusty piles on street corners.
Ocean temperatures in northern waters typically hit their coldest point around January 30, making the water denser and heavier than summer seawater.

January 30 is when many evergreen trees like pine and spruce start secretly preparing for spring by moving nutrients from their roots up to their branches, even though it looks like nothing is happening.
When there's a full moon on January 30, it often appears extra bright and white because winter air typically has less humidity to make it look yellow or orange.
